title: Sphagneticola brachycarpa
taxonomy: Sphagneticola brachycarpa is a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This species is further classified into the tribe Heliantheae, a group of daisies and sunflowers native to the Americas. Sphagneticola brachycarpa is a flowering plant native to South America, and its classification reflects its relationships with other plants within this family. Further, it is sometimes considered a member of the tribe Senecioneae.
uses: Sphagneticola brachycarpa, also known as Wedelia or Sphagneticola, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. intro: Sphagneticola brachycarpa, also known as Wedelia or Mexican daisy, is a perennial herb native to Central and South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sphagneticola brachycarpa include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Sphagneticola brachycarpa is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of salves and creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
